expectations.text: Grounds the advice in the project plan: cites specific, accurate details from clearcontract_project_plan.md (phases, schema, architecture decisions, or the Out of Scope list)
expectations.evidence: Cites Out of Scope section 9, the token-based public page decision, the customers collection not being an auth collection, Phase 4/6 sizing, and the 4-6 week phased plan.

expectations.text: Notices that customer accounts/logins are explicitly deferred in the plan's Out of Scope list (section 9)
expectations.evidence: "'Customer accounts/logins' is explicitly in the Out of Scope list (section 9)... you'd be reversing a deliberate decision, so the bar is evidence, not vibes."

expectations.text: Presents at least two genuine alternatives to the user's proposal (a defended 'defer / do nothing' counts as one)
expectations.evidence: Three options: defer entirely; tokenized /c/[token] customer page with no login (~1-2 days); full login portal (5-8 days). Each argued on its merits.

expectations.text: Commits to a single clear recommendation rather than a neutral pros/cons list
expectations.evidence: "My recommendation: option 1 now, with option 2 as the earmarked upgrade... The login version I'd say no to outright." Includes the evidence trigger for revisiting (customers asking to resend links).

expectations.text: Weighs build cost concretely for a solo part-time developer (time estimates or specific complexity costs like new collections/hooks)
expectations.evidence: "Honestly 5-8 days for one part-time dev, plus a permanent security surface"; token-page option costed at 1-2 days including tests, one field, one hook endpoint, one page.

notes: Also checked git state (zero commits) and used it in the argument, per the skill's 'ground yourself' step.
Closed by offering to record the decision in docs/decisions.md, per the skill.
